definition.en: An explicit boundary within which a particular domain model is defined and applicable. All terms of the Ubiquitous Language have a specific meaning within this boundary.
A common, rigorous language built between developers and domain experts within a Bounded Context, used in all conversations, code, tests, and documentation.
A visual representation of the relationships and integration patterns between different Bounded Contexts in a system.
A cluster of associated domain objects treated as a single unit for data changes, with an Aggregate Root controlling all access and enforcing business invariants.
A domain object defined by a thread of continuity and identity, rather than by its attributes. Two entities with the same attributes are still distinct if they have different identities.
A domain object that describes some characteristic or attribute but carries no concept of identity. Value Objects are immutable and are equal when all their attributes are equal.
Something that happened in the domain that domain experts care about. Domain Events are the core artefact of Event Storming, represented by orange sticky notes.
A middleware component that receives events from producers and routes them to interested consumers. Examples include Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, and AWS EventBridge.
